source.notes: Optimus _ M7 - 2026_06_02 14_59 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Reconnection call roughly five to six months after an initial February intro, in which Max reintroduced Optimus with a new per-seat model ($175/seat, annual term, 10% discount for full annual payment), a bundled CRM, and its core differentiator: proprietary freight/lane data pulled via OCR scanning of factoring invoices and bills of lading rather than just contacts. Isaac engaged actively, pressure-tested the tool against his own Texas/Houston lanes, and verbally committed to starting a one-seat trial for himself before bringing the wider team on. Next step is undated: Max to send a summary/proposal, Isaac to present it to his team and get back.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max sounded prepared (he had reviewed the prior contact history) and responded directly and in real time to Isaac's questions rather than reciting a script, driving a live demo off the buyer's own prompts; his pitch variation (8.03 st) was healthy despite arousal fading over the call.

evidence.quote: We got stuff all the way back to February.
It's the freight data that we have.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could explain it in one sentence: Optimus gives reps proprietary, OCR-sourced freight and lane data — what shippers are actually moving — not just contacts anyone can buy, which is a defensible edge. It is concrete though light on quantified proof (e.g., data volume or freshness).

evidence.quote: we do the OCR scanning for them.

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tailored to Isaac's actual freight network: when he mentioned Texas/Houston, Max immediately filtered the tool to Houston-origin, small-to-mid-size companies and surfaced a matching lane rather than showing a generic view.

evidence.quote: Yeah. So, I just selected Houston within 25 miles as the origin.
I'm going to get rid of enterprise companies. So, let's just say we do smallmediumsiz companies.

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked few discovery questions (the buyer drove most of the questioning) and never dug into team size, freight volume, budget, or current pain; he also accepted a vague, undated next step instead of pinning a trial start or follow-up date after Isaac self-qualified into a one-seat trial.

evidence.quote: and then I'll get right back with you.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: When a buyer self-qualifies (as Isaac did with the one-seat trial), immediately lock a concrete dated next step out loud — e.g., 'I'll send the summary today; can we get your seat provisioned and book a 15-minute kickoff this Thursday?' — instead of accepting an open-ended 'I'll get back to you.'

signals.signal: Verbal (but hedged) commitment to a one-seat trial for himself before rolling out to the team
signals.quote: I may be interested in just starting with one seat to start off for myself and just run with it before bringing the rest of

signals.signal: Buyer-owned next step: will take it to his team and requested a written summary to move forward
signals.quote: if you could just send me whatever is needed or the details of the outline or like a summary of what we talked about and then I'll get right back with you.

signals.signal: Deep, fit-testing question mapping the tool to his own lanes (Texas/Houston, SoCal outbound)
signals.quote: if I wanted to like focus on more Texas stuff, I'd be able to kind of target it in and then kind of filter it that way

signals.signal: Explicit value recognition tied to consolidation and lane insight over his current Sales Dash workflow
signals.quote: I do like that a lot more because there's more value there, you know.
